questions.question: Explain the meaning of political institutions.
questions.answer: Political institutions are organizations that help in governing a country by making and implementing decisions according to established rules.

questions.question: Why are institutions important in democratic governments?
questions.answer: Institutions ensure decisions are taken through rules, procedures and participation of different authorities.

questions.question: What was the Mandal Commission?
questions.answer: The Mandal Commission was a commission set up to identify socially and educationally backward classes and recommend measures for their advancement.

questions.question: Why was the Mandal Commission decision controversial?
questions.answer: It was controversial because it introduced 27% reservation for backward classes in government jobs, which led to widespread debates and protests.

questions.question: State two functions of Parliament.
questions.answer: Parliament makes laws and controls the government’s finances.

questions.question: Why is Parliament considered the highest law-making body?
questions.answer: Because it has the authority to create, amend or abolish laws in the country.

questions.question: Write two differences between Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
questions.answer: Lok Sabha members are directly elected while Rajya Sabha members are indirectly elected. Lok Sabha has greater power in financial matters.

questions.question: What is the role of the Lok Sabha in controlling the government?
questions.answer: Lok Sabha controls the government through questions, debates and no-confidence motions.

questions.question: What is the political executive?
questions.answer: The political executive consists of elected leaders who make important policy decisions for the government.

questions.question: What is meant by permanent executive?
questions.answer: Permanent executive refers to civil servants who assist ministers in implementing policies.

questions.question: Why do civil servants remain in office when governments change?
questions.answer: They remain in office because they are permanent administrative officials.

questions.question: State two powers of the Prime Minister.
questions.answer: The Prime Minister chairs Cabinet meetings and coordinates the work of different ministries.

questions.question: What is the Council of Ministers?
questions.answer: It is a group of ministers headed by the Prime Minister responsible for running the government.

questions.question: Explain the role of the President in India.
questions.answer: The President is the head of the state and performs functions according to the advice of the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: How is the President of India elected?
questions.answer: The President is elected by the elected members of Parliament and state legislatures.

questions.question: What is the judiciary?
questions.answer: The judiciary is the system of courts that interprets laws and settles disputes.

questions.question: Name the three levels of courts in India.
questions.answer: Supreme Court, High Courts and District Courts.

questions.question: What is judicial review?
questions.answer: Judicial review is the power of courts to examine laws and declare them unconstitutional.

questions.question: What is Public Interest Litigation?
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation is a case filed in court to protect public interest.
